functions.name: neuromeka_indycare.indycare_utils.sshpass.ssh_exec_pass
documentation:
Wrapper around openssh that allows you to send a password to
ssh/sftp/scp et al similar to sshpass.

Not super robust, but works well enough for most purposes. Typical
usage might be:

```
ssh_exec_pass('p@ssw0rd', ['ssh', 'root@1.2.3.4', 'echo hi!'])
```

```
param args

A list of args. arg[0] must be the command to run.

param capture_output

If True, suppresses output to stdout and stores
it in a buffer that is returned

returns

(retval, output)
```

nix only, tested on linux and OSX. Python 2.7 and 3.3+ compatible.

methods.name: neuromeka_indycare.IndyCAREReport.Reporter.__ssh_worker
documentation: Worker function that runs ssh_exec_pass and puts output into a queue.

- get data from robot (IndyDCP3 for v3, IndyDCP for v2)
- publish to mqtt broker
- notify when event happen (zip file, on/off camera)
